Re: 
Your Request for Written 
Advice; Our File No. 
A-83-113 
Dear Assemblyman Roos: 
You nave requested our advice as to the question of your 
possible disqual~fication from participating in decisions 
affecting SB 259 (Mello), which is currently pending before the 
Legislature. 
You have stated the question as follows: 
I have a close personal friend whom I see frequently on 
a social basis, Ms. Lorna Stevenson, who is currently 
an account executive with Traweek Investments wnich 
owns apartment un~ts in San Francisco. 
Those units may 
be affected by SB 259. 
Over the past year, we have excnanged gifts of 
comparable value on festive occasions sucn as 
Christmas. 
Any gifts whiCh I have received do not 
exceed, on a cumulative basis, $250.00 in value. 
The question I wiSh to ask tne Commission is: Am I 
precluded under the Political Reform Act or any other 
statute from voting on SB 259 if it comes before me in 
committee or on the Floor? 
We cannot advise you with regard to any statutory or other 
, 
I 
provisions of law outside the Political Reform Act.~1 
You 
should consult Legislative Counselor the Attorney General in 
that regara. 
11 
Government Code Sections 81000-91014. 
All statutory 
references are to tne Government COde unless otherwise specified. 


Honorable Mike Roos 
April 27, 1983 
Page Two 
The Political Reform Act provides that no public official 
shall make, participate in making, or use his or her official 
position to influence any official decision in which he or she 
has a financial interest. 
Section 87100. 
Financial interests 
are defined in Section 87103 and include sources of income of 
$250 or more during the preceding l2-month period. 
Gifts are 
included as income under the Political Reform Act. 
Section 
82030{a) • 
The facts which you have provided state that the value of 
any gifts which you have received from Ms. Stevenson is less 
than $250. 
Consequently, our inquiry ends there and we need not 
pursue the analysis further. 
Ms. Stevenson does not constitute 
a source of income to you and consequently is not a financial 
interest within the meaning of Section 87100. 
You have no 
conflict of interest vis a vis MS. Stevenson within the meaning 
of the Political Reform-Act:--
Should you have further questions, please do not hesitate to 
call me at (916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Sincerely,

Dear Mr. Prim: 
I am writing to ask the legal opinion of the Fair 
Political Practices Commission with respect to my 
ability to act on SB 259 (Mello) which is now 
pending before the Legislature. 
I have a close personal friend whom I see frequently 
AND CONSTt"r-U-,10NAL AMENDMENTS 
on a social basis, Ms. Lorna Stevenson, who is currently 
an account executive with Traweek Investments which owns 
apartment units in San Francisco. 
Those units may be 
affected by SB 259. 
Over the past year, we have exchanged gifts of com-
parable value on festive occasions such as Christmas. 
Any gifts which I have received do not exceed, on a 
cumulative basis, $250.00 in value. 
The question I wish to ask the Commission is: Am I 
precluded under the Political Reform Act or any other 
statute from voting on SB 259 if it comes before me 
in committee or on the Floor? 
Your prompt attention to this matter is very much 
appreciated. 
Leader

sections.question: I have a close personal friend whom I see frequently on a social basis, Ms. Lorna Stevenson, who is currently an account executive with Traweek Investments which owns apartment units in San Francisco. Those units may be affected by SB 259. Over the past year, we have exchanged gifts of comparable value on festive occasions such as Christmas. Any gifts which I have received do not exceed, on a cumulative basis, $250.00 in value. The question I wish to ask the Commission is: Am I precluded under the Political Reform Act or any other statute from voting on SB 259 if it comes before me in committee or on the Floor?
sections.conclusion: The facts which you have provided state that the value of any gifts which you have received from Ms. Stevenson is less than $250. Consequently, our inquiry ends there and we need not pursue the analysis further. Ms. Stevenson does not constitute a source of income to you and consequently is not a financial interest within the meaning of Section 87100. You have no conflict of interest vis a vis Ms. Stevenson within the meaning of the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: May State Assembly Majority Floor Leader Mike Roos vote on SB 259 despite having a close personal friend whose employer would be affected by the bill, where cumulative gifts from that friend total less than $250 over the past year?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Roos has no conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act because gifts from Stevenson totaling less than $250 do not constitute a financial interest as defined by Section 87103.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter and FPPC's written response. OCR quality varies (e.g., 'disqual~fication'). Signature on response appears illegible but identified as Robert E. Uidigh, Counsel. Clear question and conclusion sections present.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: I have a close personal friend whom I see frequently on a social basis, Ms. Lorna Stevenson, who is currently an account executive with Traweek Investments which owns apartment units in San Francisco. Those units may be affected by SB 259. Over the past year, we have exchanged gifts of comparable value on festive occasions such as Christmas. Any gifts which I have received do not exceed, on a cumulative basis, $250.00 in value. The question I wish to ask the Commission is: Am I precluded under the Political Reform Act or any other statute from voting on SB 259 if it comes before me in committee or on the Floor?

CONCLUSION: The facts which you have provided state that the value of any gifts which you have received from Ms. Stevenson is less than $250. Consequently, our inquiry ends there and we need not pursue the analysis further. Ms. Stevenson does not constitute a source of income to you and consequently is not a financial interest within the meaning of Section 87100. You have no conflict of interest vis a vis Ms. Stevenson within the meaning of the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter to Assemblyman Mike Roos regarding potential disqualification from voting on SB 259 due to a friendship with an account executive whose employer owns property potentially affected by the bill. The Commission concludes no conflict of interest exists because cumulative gifts under $250 do not constitute a reportable financial interest.
